ns2:document.ns3:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

ns2:r.ns2:t: TMPL - Product Governance - Presales Guide & Offer Material
enabling them to work out a solid
Goal of the document is to centralize information
The presales guide should contain solution guidance with commercial texts, added value, description of responsibilities, prerequisites, etc.
A first enumeration for this chapter is given
more sections can be added if needed (or sections can be deleted if irrelevant).
Sections marked with MVP, are minimal to be present when a Product is released.
Important: make sure you don’t link to individual files
; only reference to folder or other products. Filename including a versioning will change and we don’t want to make updates because these have been changed.
key checks that need to be done before selling the product
reused in offers/proposals (text is either in this document, or a reference is made to another
, but sections can be copied into another
an offer (minimal list that should be worked out by product
product mgmt. decides on which additional section
also important to use in an offer)
containing relevant text for an offer (e.g. SLAs in service description)
by the name of the targeted customer
TMPL - Product Governance - Presales Guide & Offer Material
Write down the presales checks & instructions
a living part of the document and will be updated
during the lifecycle of the product)
tandard offer sections -> minimal list that should be worked out
additional sections can be added that are important

(Insert > Quick parts > Document Property > Company) to refer to the customer throughout the entire document. In this way, the document can quickly be personalized for specific customers by presales (see presales README)
informative text in each section of this template
When the first version is made, delete this
Version tracking can be done by using the document history page
The Cegeka persons that should be contacted in case of questions (e.g.
technical vs sales vs prod mgmt.)
A list of mandatory checks that need to be done before selling the product.
mentioned in the service description. Goal of this section is
make sure the additional information, prerequisite SKU’s (for example monitoring) or linked SKU’s
(for example different flavours of an offering)
Which set of SKUs are optional, which mandatory,
link to the right SKU section in the service description
are always sold together with this solution (e.g. service delivery manager not part of the
Which information is required to sell a certain product? Which questions and information do we need
Short introduction giving context to the described solution (e.g. Cyberattacks are ubiquitous
Summary of the client's current state or challenges (if applicable). Goals and desired outcomes. Key pain points to be addressed.
of the components of the Product (hardware, software, network,
. Provide specific standards, tools and best practices.
The text should provide the “big picture” for a customer and can
if the solution can find a fit
in their business. Goal is not to provide a
and how this solution can solve these needs
Success stories or case studies of similar implementations.
Give each need a separate subtitle
What sets Cegeka apart from competitors:
Which responsibilities do we expect that the <customer> will pick up.
When is Cegeka considering the solution as ready? What is the timeline for acceptance?
the service and are usually non-technical or lightly detailed.
a broad audience and to communicate essential expectations.
Within this document more details are addressed
monitoring mechanisms, escalation paths, and penalties/rewards for breaches
Include operational-level SLAs tied to specific parts of the solution, such as cloud infrastructure response times, data recovery objectives, or API performance
precise technical, operational, and contractual commitments
commitments and how SLAs align with the technical design.
"Critical support requests will receive a response within 2 hours."
"API latency will not exceed 200ms for 95% of requests, measured monthly."
"Data backup recovery point objective (RPO) is 4 hours, and recovery time objective (RTO) is 24 hours."
costing elements required to build a price towards the customer. This can be for example specific license information,
When translating the costing input to a price, are there considerations to think
What is the breakdown of the SKU?
documented in the service offering, will help to set the direction. But often both inputs need to come together into
to complete the deliverables in a bid.
contains attractive text/figures and can be found
The service description mainly contains legally, fully inclusive text, but can still be used in offers when this is needed (e.g. for SLAs). The service description can be found
